data.flavor_text: "Life is vivid; the pigments I use should be too."

data.name: Additive Evolution

When this enchantment enters, create a 0/0 green and blue Fractal creature token. Put three +1/+1 counters on it.
At the beginning of combat on your turn, put a +1/+1 counter on target creature you control. It gains vigilance until end of turn.
data.flavor_text:
"Numbers have no limits. Why should nature?"
—Emil, Quandrix fourth-year

data.name: Ajani's Response
data.oracle_text:
This spell costs {3} less to cast if it targets a tapped creature.
Destroy target creature.
data.flavor_text: Ajani no longer wanted to swing his axe in battle, but nothing came easier to him than defending those who needed protection.

data.name: Ambitious Augmenter
data.oracle_text:
Increment (Whenever you cast a spell, if the amount of mana you spent is greater than this creature's power or toughness, put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.)
When this creature dies, if it had one or more counters on it, create a 0/0 green and blue Fractal creature token, then put this creature's counters on that token.

data.name: Ancestral Anger
data.oracle_text:
Target creature gains trample and gets +X/+0 until end of turn, where X is 1 plus the number of cards named Ancestral Anger in your graveyard.
Draw a card.
data.flavor_text: An incautious Lorehold student may learn more about the unbound rage of the Blood Age than they wanted to.

data.name: Antiquities on the Loose
data.oracle_text:
Create two 2/2 red and white Spirit creature tokens. Then if this spell was cast from anywhere other than your hand, put a +1/+1 counter on each Spirit you control.
Flashback {4}{W}{W} (You may cast this card from your graveyard for its flashback cost. Then exile it.)
data.flavor_text: Raising spirits is easier than taming them.

data.name: Applied Geometry
data.oracle_text: Create a token that's a copy of target non-Aura permanent you control, except it's a 0/0 Fractal creature in addition to its other types. Put six +1/+1 counters on it.
data.flavor_text: "I know the prompt was to replicate a scurrid, but I took some creative liberties with my version."

data.name: Arcane Omens
data.oracle_text: Converge — Target player discards X cards, where X is the number of colors of mana spent to cast this spell.
data.flavor_text: Lluwen suddenly felt that he was seeing something very dangerous. The crackling magic, the thunderous chanting—the urge to turn and run replaced all his curiosity.

data.name: Archaic's Agony
data.oracle_text: Converge — Archaic's Agony deals X damage to target creature, where X is the number of colors of mana spent to cast this spell. Exile cards from the top of your library equal to the excess damage dealt to that creature this way. You may play those cards until the end of your next turn.
data.flavor_text: Its past became present became paradox.

data.name: Ark of Hunger
data.oracle_text:
Whenever one or more cards leave your graveyard, this artifact deals 1 damage to each opponent and you gain 1 life.
{T}: Mill a card. You may play that card this turn.
data.flavor_text: Few relics survived the turmoil of the Blood Age; those that did are powerful, dangerous, or both.

data.name: Arnyn, Deathbloom Botanist
data.oracle_text:
Deathtouch
Whenever a creature you control with power or toughness 1 or less dies, target opponent loses 2 life and you gain 2 life.
data.flavor_text: "My lovelies need meat, just like any other carnivore."

Flying

At the beginning of combat on your turn, exile up to one target card from a graveyard.
data.flavor_text: You can always tell who just left Professor Osgir's lecture on practical restoration.

data.name: Aziza, Mage Tower Captain
data.oracle_text: Whenever you cast an instant or sorcery spell, you may tap three untapped creatures you control. If you do, copy that spell. You may choose new targets for the copy.
data.flavor_text: "Good play, team! Now let's run it again until it's perfect!"

data.name: Banishing Betrayal
data.oracle_text: Return target nonland permanent to its owner's hand. Surveil 1. (Look at the top card of your library. You may put it into your graveyard.)
data.flavor_text: One simple push and Jadzi fell into the churning swirl of magic. Tam thought it would be harder—wished that it had been harder.

Increment (Whenever you cast a spell, if the amount of mana you spent is greater than this creature's power or toughness, put a +1/+1 counter on this creature.)

"You will hear rumors that licking lumarets grants prophetic visions. That is untrue. Please refrain from licking the lumarets."
—Witherbloom student handbook

• Discard your hand, then draw cards equal to the number of cards discarded this way.

Deathtouch
{1}{B}: This creature gets +1/+1 until end of turn.

data.name: Flow State

If you gained life this turn, you may instead reveal two creature and/or land cards from among them and put them into your hand. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in a random order.

data.flavor_text: Not until Lluwen watched Professor Fel in action did he truly understand just how closely life and death were intertwined.

card_faces.name: Grave Researcher
card_faces.oracle_text: At the beginning of your upkeep, surveil 1. Then if there are three or more creature cards in your graveyard, this creature becomes prepared. (While it's prepared, you may cast a copy of its spell. Doing so unprepares it.)

card_faces.oracle_text: Put target creature card from a graveyard onto the battlefield under your control. You lose life equal to that card's mana value.

{T}: Add {C}.

data.name: Group Project
data.oracle_text:
Create a 2/2 red and white Spirit creature token.

"Never be afraid to ask for help. Success is much sweeter when it's shared."
—Quintorius Kand

data.name: Growth Curve
data.oracle_text: Put a +1/+1 counter on target creature you control, then double the number of +1/+1 counters on that creature.
data.flavor_text: "There is no force stronger or more daunting than that of exponential growth."

data.name: Homesickness

"We know you're working hard. Dress warm and get enough sleep. Dad says hi!"
—Love, Mom

"Theory? Meet practice."
—Tam, Quandrix second-year
